introSection: Wilderness Run is a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened in 1979 and was called Jr. Gemini, after the nearby Gemini. It was the first roller coaster to be built by Intamin.

On August 27, 2013, it was announced that Jr. Gemini would be renamed Wilderness Run and its entrance moved into the Camp Snoopy area for the 2014 season.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Riders over 54 inches (137 cm) must be accompanied by a child.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Green track and brown supports. The ride used to have blue track and supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Currently, the train is painted in yellow and orange. In the past, it has been painted in rainbow colors and entirely blue.

fullText:
Wilderness Run is a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA.

The ride opened in 1979 and was called Jr. Gemini, after the nearby Gemini. It was the first roller coaster to be built by Intamin.

On August 27, 2013, it was announced that Jr. Gemini would be renamed Wilderness Run and its entrance moved into the Camp Snoopy area for the 2014 season.

Riders over 54 inches (137 cm) must be accompanied by a child.

Green track and brown supports. The ride used to have blue track and supports.

Currently, the train is painted in yellow and orange. In the past, it has been painted in rainbow colors and entirely blue.
